title: geum urbanum var. hispidum
intro: Geum urbanum var. hispidum, also known as rough avens, is a type of flowering plant in the rose family, characterized by its distinctive, hairy stems and leaves. Traditionally used in herbal medicine, rough avens has been found to possess antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which are beneficial for treating various health issues. The plant's medicinal properties are said to provide relief from digestive issues, such as nausea and diarrhea, as well as skin conditions like eczema and acne, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory effects. Rough avens can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, which are commonly used in traditional medicine to harness its health benefits.
